title: BeatDetector
description: Real-time tempo + beat tracker. Splits work between the audio-rendering thread (an AudioWorklet that runs onset detection, tempogram analysis, and parallel 3/4 and 4/4 posterior estimation) and the main thread (this class — receives beats, fires Signals, handles configuration and source routing). Accepts a wide range of BeatDetectorSources — a bus, an individual Voice, a raw MediaStream, or any AudioNode — and exposes a Signal for each notable event: - BeatDetector.onBeat — every detected beat - BeatDetector.onDownbeat — first beat of each bar - BeatDetector.onBarStart — bar boundary - BeatDetector.onTempoChange — when the tracked BPM changes - BeatDetector.onBeatPredicted — look-ahead schedule notice Early beats arrive with low latency tagged `status: 'provisional'` (after a short `minSettlingMs` warm-up, default 400 ms) for snappy visual reactivity, then promote to `status: 'locked'` once the tempo grid is trustworthy — a single BeatDetector.onBeat signal carries both via BeatInfo.status. Set `emitProvisionalBeats: false` to receive only locked beats. Time-signature detection (3/4 vs 4/4) is on by default; lock to 4/4 by setting `enableTimeSignatureDetection: false`.

sections.title: Import
paragraphs: Real-time tempo + beat tracker. Splits work between the audio-rendering thread (an AudioWorklet that runs onset detection, tempogram analysis, and parallel 3/4 and 4/4 posterior estimation) and the main thread (this class — receives beats, fires Signals, handles configuration and source routing).

Accepts a wide range of BeatDetectorSources — a bus, an individual Voice, a raw MediaStream, or any AudioNode — and exposes a Signal for each notable event: - BeatDetector.onBeat — every detected beat - BeatDetector.onDownbeat — first beat of each bar - BeatDetector.onBarStart — bar boundary - BeatDetector.onTempoChange — when the tracked BPM changes - BeatDetector.onBeatPredicted — look-ahead schedule notice

Early beats arrive with low latency tagged `status: 'provisional'` (after a short `minSettlingMs` warm-up, default 400 ms) for snappy visual reactivity, then promote to `status: 'locked'` once the tempo grid is trustworthy — a single BeatDetector.onBeat signal carries both via BeatInfo.status. Set `emitProvisionalBeats: false` to receive only locked beats. Time-signature detection (3/4 vs 4/4) is on by default; lock to 4/4 by setting `enableTimeSignatureDetection: false`.

members.name: subdivisionPhase
members.description: Phase 0..1 within a subdivision of the current beat. division is the number of subdivisions per beat: 2 for 8th notes, 4 for 16th notes, 3 for triplets. Use to drive sub-beat-resolution effects: const sixteenth = clock.subdivisionPhase(4); if (sixteenth < 0.05) flash();

members.name: barPulseHalfLife
members.description: Half-life for the barPulse envelope. Mutable; default 0.3.

members.name: justBeatWindow
members.description: Time window for justBeat. Mutable; default 0.03 (30ms).

members.name: pulseHalfLife
members.description: Half-life in seconds for the pulse envelope. Mutable; default 0.15. Smaller values give a snappier pulse, larger values a longer afterglow.

members.name: barPulse
members.description: Like pulse but resets on downbeats and decays per barPulseHalfLife. Useful for emphasizing the first beat of each bar (e.g. brighter flash on "1" vs "2,3,4").

members.name: justBeat
members.description: True for the visual frame(s) within justBeatWindow seconds of a beat onset. Use for one-shot triggers (strobe flash, particle burst, sample retrigger). Default window 30ms covers a typical 60fps frame.

members.name: pulse
members.description: 0..1 envelope, peaks at 1.0 the moment a beat fires and halves every pulseHalfLife seconds. Drives "pulse on the beat" visuals with a single multiplication: sprite.scale = 1 + clock.pulse * 0.3.

members.name: secondsSinceLastBeat
members.description: Seconds elapsed since the most recent beat, derived from beatPhase and tempo. Returns 0 when the detector hasn't locked yet.
